title: WSU Online Application 2026 | Walter Sisulu University Apply
metaTitle: WSU Online Application 2026 | Walter Sisulu University
metaDescription: Apply to Walter Sisulu University (WSU) online for 2026/2027. APS 18–28+. Application fee R130. Full guide: how to apply, deadlines, APS requirements.
content:
WSU Online Application 2026

Walter Sisulu University (WSU) is a comprehensive university with campuses in the Eastern Cape, serving students from Mthatha, Butterworth, Komani (Queenstown), and East London. WSU offers diplomas, degrees, and postgraduate qualifications at accessible entry requirements, making it a popular choice for Eastern Cape students.

WSU Application Key Dates 2026

EventDate (Approximate)
2027 Applications OpenApril 1, 2026
Application Closing DateSeptember 30, 2026
Results NotificationNovember–December 2026
Academic Year StartsFebruary 2027

How to Apply to WSU Online

  1. Visit the WSU website: www.wsu.ac.za
  2. Click "Apply Online" from the Prospective Students menu
  3. Create a student applicant profile with your personal information
  4. Complete the application form: Select your preferred programme and campus
  5. Upload supporting documents: Certified ID copy, Grade 11 or Grade 12 results, proof of residence
  6. Pay the application fee: R130 (approximately) — check the current fee on the WSU website
  7. Submit and save your reference number

WSU APS Requirements by Faculty

FacultyMinimum APSKey Subject Requirements
Health Sciences (Medicine MBChB)28+Mathematics 4+, Physical Sciences 4+, Life Sciences 4+, English 4+
Health Sciences (Nursing)22+Life Sciences 4+, English 3+, Mathematics 3+
Science, Engineering & Technology24+Mathematics 4+, Physical Sciences 4+, English 3+
Business, Management Sciences & Law20+English 3+, Mathematics 3+ or Mathematical Literacy 4+
Education20+English 4+, subject specialisation applies
Humanities, Social Sciences & Law18+English 3+, any five subjects

Use our free APS Calculator to confirm your eligibility for WSU.

WSU Campuses

CampusLocationMain Faculties
Mthatha (main)Mthatha, Eastern CapeHealth Sciences (Medicine), Education, Humanities
ButterworthButterworth, Eastern CapeScience & Engineering, Management Sciences
Komani (Queenstown)Komani, Eastern CapeEducation, Management Sciences
East LondonEast London, Eastern CapeManagement Sciences, Business

WSU Medicine (MBChB) Requirements

WSU's Medical School (Faculty of Health Sciences) is one of the few in South Africa that offers Medicine at a relatively lower APS threshold, making it accessible to Eastern Cape students. Requirements:

  • Minimum APS: 28+
  • Mathematics: Level 4 (50%+)
  • Physical Sciences: Level 4 (50%+)
  • Life Sciences: Level 4 (50%+)
  • English: Level 4 (50%+)

NSFAS at WSU

WSU is a NSFAS-accredited institution. Students from households with income below R350,000/year may qualify for NSFAS funding (tuition, accommodation, and living allowance). Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

Need to improve your APS for WSU? StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps Grade 10–12 students improve their marks across all subjects. Start free today.
quickAnswer: Apply to Walter Sisulu University (WSU) online at www.wsu.ac.za. Applications for 2027 open April 1, 2026 and close September 30, 2026. Application fee is approximately R130. Minimum APS varies from 18 (Humanities) to 28+ (Medicine).

faqs.question: How do I apply to WSU online?
faqs.answer: Apply online at www.wsu.ac.za. Click 'Apply Online', create an applicant profile, select your programme and campus, upload your certified ID and academic results, and pay the R130 application fee. Applications open around April 1 each year.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS for WSU?
faqs.answer: WSU minimum APS ranges from 18 (Humanities and Social Sciences) to 28+ (Medicine/Health Sciences). Management Sciences requires 20+, Engineering requires 24+, and Nursing requires 22+. Use the free APS Calculator at /aps-calculator to check your eligibility.

faqs.question: When do WSU applications close?
faqs.answer: WSU application closing date for 2027 intake is approximately September 30, 2026. Apply early — competitive programmes like Medicine may close earlier. Visit www.wsu.ac.za to verify the exact closing date.

faqs.question: What is WSU's application fee?
faqs.answer: Walter Sisulu University charges approximately R130 for an undergraduate application. This fee is non-refundable. Check the current application fee at www.wsu.ac.za before applying.
